brand_focus: Cooking tips, recipes, and marinades with AI-styled ingredient photography. Audience is Europe-first, US a strong second. Goal: grow followers fast-but-safe by making every post highly shareable (sent to a friend) and saveable.

slots.note: Thu ~12:17 CET — w3-12 is the single highest-reach slot (avg 3.0); sauce is the top-reach theme (avg 2.5). Best slot + best theme retained. Minute shifted from 41 for jitter.
Tue ~18:37 CET — w1-18 is second-best slot (avg 2.3); marinade is second-best theme (avg 2.3). Strong pairing retained. Minute shifted from 22.
Sat ~11:53 CET — w5-11 avg reach 2.0, on par with most slots. Technique (avg 2.0) tested here; 3 posts now give enough signal to keep but not elevate yet. Minute shifted for jitter.

Mon ~19:14 CET — w0-19 avg reach 2.0 is solid. Doubling sauce (top theme) to gather more signal on its save/send potential across a second slot and day. Quick-dinner retired: 2 posts, weakest reach (1.5), no engagement lift.

content_plan.sauce: A versatile sauce, glaze, or dressing built on a memorisable ratio (e.g. 2-1-1 fat/acid/flavour). Slide 1: hero shot of finished sauce on a clean, beautiful surface — no text, numbers, or labels on the image. Slide 2: another beauty angle showing the sauce in context (spooned over protein, pooling on a board). Slides 3-4: show the same sauce applied to two very different foods (e.g. grilled fish AND roasted veg) to maximise perceived versatility and DM sends. Final slide: close-up texture shot. ALL ratio, quantities, and '3 ways to use this tonight' content go in the caption only — never on the image.
content_plan.marinade: One complete marinade built around a memorable ratio (e.g. 3-2-1 oil/acid/flavour). Slide 1: bold hero shot of the finished marinated protein — no text on image. Slide 2: ingredients laid flat on a clean surface in an aesthetically arranged flat-lay, nothing labelled. Slides 3-4: process shots — protein submerged, colour change after marinating — purely visual, no overlaid text. Final slide: finished dish plated beautifully. ALL ratios, marinating times, technique notes, save CTAs, and send prompts go in the caption only.
content_plan.technique: One practical, slightly surprising kitchen technique that produces a visible, striking result. Slide 1: dramatic outcome shot — the 'after' at its most visually compelling, no text. Slide 2: close-up of the key action (knife angle, fold, sear surface) — purely photographic. Slides 3-4: side-by-side or sequential shots showing contrast (wrong vs right result) using only visual difference, no labels or text overlaid. Final slide: clean beauty shot of the finished result. ALL explanatory language, the rule being demonstrated, and send prompts go in the caption only.

caption_strategy: Every caption should open with a search-friendly first line that names the dish, technique, or sauce plainly (e.g. 'Lemon-herb chicken marinade — 3-2-1 ratio') so it surfaces in Instagram keyword search. Follow with the full ratio or recipe in scannable lines, because text-heavy save-bait in the caption is the primary mechanism for saves and sends on a young account with tiny reach. Include one explicit send hook mid-caption ('Send this to whoever always over-marinades') and close with a save prompt ('Save before you need it'). Write in a warm, confident, slightly conversational tone — one person sharing a trick they genuinely love. Keep total caption under 220 words; front-load all useful information before the hashtags.
hashtag_strategy: Use 3–5 hashtags maximum, placed at the very end of the caption after a line break. Prioritise mid-size niche tags (300k–3M posts) that exactly match the content: e.g. #marinaderecipe, #saucefromscratch, #cookingtechnique, #weeknightcooking, #flavourtips. Avoid mega-generic tags (#food, #recipe) — they bury young accounts instantly. Rotate one slot to a smaller hyper-niche tag (<150k posts, e.g. #brineandbloom or #acidbalance) to build a findable owned space. Do not repeat the same set across consecutive posts; vary at least two tags each time to probe which clusters drive non-follower reach.
learnings: All engagement metrics (sends, saves, follows) are zero across 10 posts — reach numbers are tiny (1–3 people), almost certainly friends or staff, not real algorithmic distribution. No theme or slot has proven superiority on the signals that matter. Quick-dinner is retired on weakest reach. Sauce leads on reach and gets a second weekly slot to test saves/sends more quickly. The priority for the next cycle is proving save-bait and send-bait caption mechanics work at all before scaling cadence. Hold at 4 posts/week; do not ramp until at least one post shows saves_per_reach > 0.
